tracks.acceptance_route: Create an external_review_intake_record accepted_review only after capacity and attribution checks; route findings to proof tasks, claim narrowing, blockers, or explicit rejection. Review input alone cannot promote support.
tracks.non_claim: Packet readiness is not formal-methods review or proof adequacy.

tracks.acceptance_route: Record accepted review input separately from evidence; route governance failures to architecture changes, adversarial cases, residuals, blockers, or explicit rejection. No automatic support movement.
tracks.non_claim: Packet readiness is not a safety audit, governance approval, or deployed control evidence.

tracks.acceptance_route: Record systems/editorial findings as review input, then route to experiment design, product contract, chapter revision, reader review, release blocker, or explicit rejection. No automatic artifact approval.
tracks.non_claim: Packet readiness is not systems validation, publication approval, or reader-release approval.

non_claims: No named specialist reviewer is assigned or required for prepublication completion.
No specialist packet will be delivered through prepublication outreach; packets are preserved for optional post-publication use.
No independent review result, evidence transition, or artifact approval exists.
